steps.title: Use context to define the word
steps.content: Fox-Foot allows no "trace of their passing to be left." He is trying to prevent pursuers from discovering their route. Whatever "trace" means here, it is something a pursuer could detect — the marks or signs that would reveal they passed through.

steps.title: Test each choice against that meaning
steps.content:
• Choice A — Evidence: Signs or proof of passage — exactly what Fox-Foot wants to eliminate so pursuers cannot track them. ✓
  • Choice B — Blemish: A flaw or stain on an object — doesn't fit the idea of leaving detectable signs of travel. ✗
  • Choice C — Amount: A quantity — no sense in which "no amount of their passing" fits this sentence. ✗
  • Choice D — Sketch: A drawing — unrelated to the act of traveling through a location. ✗
That is the correct answer.
